subject: DevOps
title: ELK Stack for Logging
description: A quick win: understand ELK Stack for Logging.

hook.text: Millions of log lines per minute. You need to search them in seconds. ELK makes it possible.

buildup.text: Elasticsearch stores and indexes logs. Logstash processes them. Kibana visualizes them.

discovery.text: Filebeat ships logs from servers to Elasticsearch. Lightweight and easy to deploy everywhere.

twist.text: ELK is powerful but resource-hungry. Elasticsearch clusters need care and feeding.

climax.text: Grafana Loki is a lighter option. It indexes labels only, not full text. Cheaper to run.

punchline.text: ELK for power. Loki for simplicity. Choose your trade-off.

quiz.question: What role does Elasticsearch play in the ELK stack?
options: It stores and indexes log data for fast searching
